nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 美国鸟类 (书)
nodes.summary: 美国鸟类 (书)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括毛脚鵟、Bewick's wren，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦
nodes.summary: 约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括毛脚鵟、Willow flycatcher、Bell's vireo等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Bewick's wren
nodes.summary: Bewick's wren（Bewick's wren，学名 Thryomanes bewickii），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于约翰·詹姆斯·奥杜邦、美国鸟类 (书)，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括农药污染、栖息地破坏。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Sclater]], 1862 | species = bewickii | authority = (Audubon, 1827) | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = 1–2 dozen living, 2 recently extinct; see article text | synonyms = *Thryomanes leucophrys *Thryothorus bewickii *Thryothorus brevicauda *Thryothorus brevicaudus *Thryothorus bairdi | range_map = Thryomanes bewickii map.svg | range_map_caption = }} thumb|right|Illustration from John James Audubon|Audubon's [[The Birds of America]] Bewick's wren (Thryomanes bewickii) is a wren native to North America

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Sclater]], 1862 | species = bewickii | authority = (Audubon, 1827) | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = 1–2 dozen living, 2 recently extinct; see article text | synonyms = *Thryomanes leucophrys *Thryothorus bewickii *Thryothorus brevicauda *Thryothorus brevicaudus *Thryothorus bairdi | range_map = Thryomanes bewickii map.svg | range_map_caption = }} thumb|right|Illustration from John James Audubon|Audubon's [[The Birds of America]] Bewick's wren (Thryomanes bewickii) is a wren native to North America

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Eastern populations, prior to their decline, used to migrate from its northern range to the Gulf Coast. The preferred habitat of Bewick's wren is that of arid open woodlands and brush-filled areas such as hillsides and uplands, but will reside in humid areas locally (Subtropical and Temperate zones). They are more common than house wrens in drier habitats, such as those found in the Southwest. In California, Bewick's wrens inhabit a shrubland area called chaparral

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Despite this classification, no breeding pairs of Bewick's wrens are known to remain in Maryland. In 2014, the North American Bird Conservation Initiative placed the eastern Bewick's wren on its watch list. Several theories have been proposed to explain its decline in its eastern range, including pesticide use and competition from other bird species. The most likely reason seems to be competition from house wrens

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: House wrens will destroy both the nests and eggs of Bewick's wrens. The reforestation of once open land has also negatively impacted the eastern Bewick's wrens. In California, habitat loss due to development has impacted Bewick's wren
